message:
fix(tests): Fix host_selector unit tests (bd-3op)

- Change yq stub to use 'concurrency' field matching actual code
- Fix test isolation by cleaning locks between tests
- Fix jq precedence in queue status test assertion
- All 11 tests now passing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -101,7 +101,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-3je","title":"E2E: dsr quality (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr quality (real behavior)

## Purpose
Exercise quality gate execution using real commands (true/false/sleep) and real repos.d config.

## Approach
- Create temp repos config with simple check commands.
- Run `dsr quality <tool>` and verify results and exit codes.
- Validate --json output schema and stderr separation.

## Logging
- Log the check commands executed and timing.
- On failure, dump stdout/stderr.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pass/fail/skip behaviors are correct.
- [ ] Durations recorded in output.
- [ ] --json output schema-valid.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:56:57.748351674Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:08.395739440Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3je","depends_on_id":"bd-36r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:07.038078100Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3je","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:08.395293210Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3kr","title":"Implement dsr config command and configuration management","description":"# dsr Configuration Management

Centralize dsr configuration. Config at ~/.config/dsr/ including config.yaml, repos.yaml, hosts.yaml. Commands: dsr config show/edit/validate/init. Environment overrides via DSR_* vars.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Config loaded at startup
- [ ] Environment overrides work
- [ ] Validation catches errors","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:22:57.697466261Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T14:23:06.737659325Z","source_repo":".","deleted_at":"2026-01-30T14:23:06.737652281Z","deleted_by":"ubuntu","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3lo","title":"EPIC: Real-Behavior Test Suite (No Mocks)","description":"# EPIC: Real-Behavior Test Suite (No Mocks)

## Purpose
Establish full unit + E2E coverage using real behavior (no mocks/fakes), with detailed logging that makes failures immediately diagnosable.

## Non-Negotiables
- **No mocks/fakes** in unit or E2E tests.
- Use **real behavior** and real binaries where possible.
- If external deps are missing (gh auth, minisign, docker, act), tests must **skip with explicit log** explaining what to install/configure.
- **Stdout is data**, stderr is human logs; test harness must capture both and print on failure.

## Success Criteria
- All src/ modules have unit tests that exercise real code paths.
- Every CLI command has an E2E script with structured logging.
- Skips are explicit and actionable (never silent).
- JSON output + stream separation validated for all -{"id":"bd-3op","title":"Unit tests for host_selector.sh module","description":"# Unit Tests for host_selector.sh Module

## Module Overview
host_selector.sh provides host selection and concurrency management:
- `select_host` - Choose optimal host for target platform
- `host_queue_enqueue` - Add build to host queue
- `host_queue_dequeue` - Get next build from queue
- `host_concurrency_check` - Verify host under limit
- `host_capability_match` - Match target to host capabilities

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Host Selection Tests
- Select Linux host for linux/amd64 target
- Select macOS host for darwin/arm64 target
- Select Windows host for windows/amd64 target
- Fallback when preferred host unhealthy
- Error when no capable host available

### 2. Capability Matching Tests
- Host with `capabilities: [rust, go]` matches Go project
- Host without required capability rejected
- Multiple capable hosts returns highest priority

### 3. Concurrency Tests
- Respect per-host concurrency limit
- Queue builds when at capacity
- Dequeue in FIFO order
- Track running builds per host

### 4. Health Integration Tests
- Skip unhealthy hosts in selection
- Re-evaluate after health change
- Log selection rationale

## Test Approach
- Use temp directory for state files
- Mock host health status
- Test deterministic selection (same inputs → same output)
- Verify JSON output format","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:51.224832309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:53:34.069270206Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3op","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:51.224832309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

## Test Approach
- Use temp directory for state files
- Mock host health status

- Verify JSON output format","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:51.224832309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T21:49:55.754148138Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T21:49:55.754097062Z","close_reason":"Fixed yq stub to use 'concurrency' field, fixed test isolation, fixed jq precedence in queue status test. All 11 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3op","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:51.224832309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3py","title":"No-mocks compliance audit + refactor","description":"# No-Mocks Compliance Audit + Refactor

## Purpose
Remove or replace any mocked/stubbed behavior in tests with real behavior or explicit skip logic.

## Targets (initial list)
- Existing tests that stub binaries (e.g., yq, gh, ssh) or override core functions.
- Integration tests that rely on fake GH responses.
- Any helper that simulates behavior instead of invoking real code paths.

## Approach
- Prefer **real executions** against local temp fixtures or public GH data.
- If a dependency is missing (gh auth, minisign, docker/act), **skip with log**.
- Document required environment variables for tests that need credentials.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No test uses fake binaries or mocked APIs.
- [ ] All skips log a clear reason and install/auth steps.
- [ ] Updated tests remain deterministic and fast.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:54:49.933280911Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:22.680591401Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3py","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:19.202560842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3py","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:17.550410970Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3py","depends_on_id":"bd-3cn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:22.680114122Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3py","depends_on_id":"bd-3op","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:20.899404867Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3py","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:15.282284249Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3qc","title":"E2E: dsr signing (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr signing (real behavior)

## Purpose
Validate signing command using real minisign when available.

## Approach
- Create temp artifact file.
- If minisign missing, skip with install guidance.
- Run `dsr signing init` in temp XDG config dir.
- Sign and verify the artifact via CLI.

## Logging
- Log key paths and artifact path.
- On failure, dump stdout/stderr.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Keypair created with correct permissions.
- [ ] Sign + verify succeeds on real file.
- [ ] --json output schema-valid where supported.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:56:46.940700748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:05.210980058Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3qc","depends_on_id":"bd-1gm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:03.115548912Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3qc","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:05.209756292Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-8cg","title":"Update CLI contract + schemas for new commands","description":"# Update CLI Contract + Schemas for New Commands

## Purpose
Keep the CLI contract and JSON schemas authoritative as new commands/features land (status/report, prune, repos validate/discover, config migrate, fallback output).

## Scope
- Update `docs/CLI_CONTRACT.md` with new subcommands, flags, and example JSON payloads.
- Add/extend schema files:
  - `schemas/status-details.json`
  - `schemas/report-details.json` (or reuse status if identical)
  - `schemas/prune-details.json`
  - `schemas/repos-details.json`
  - `schemas/config-details.json`
  - `schemas/fallback-details.json`
- Ensure the envelope schema includes `schema_version` (additive) and stays backward compatible.
- Align error codes and stream-separation guidance for the new commands.

## Design Notes
- Prefer additive schema changes (no breaking removals).
- Keep names consistent (`status` vs `report`) across docs, schemas, and CLI help.
- Include explicit `run_id`, `duration_ms`, `warnings`, and `errors` fields in all new schemas.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CLI_CONTRACT documents status/report, prune, repos validate/discover, config validate/migrate, and fallback outputs.
- [ ] New schema files exist and validate against example fixtures.
- [ ] Schema changes are additive and compatible with existing envelope.
- [ ] Examples include both success and error cases for each new command.

## Test Plan
- [ ] Add fixtures for each new schema and validate with `test_json_schemas.sh` (ajv/jq paths).
- [ ] CI validates schema changes with strict checks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:49:46.083055419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T16:05:36.575837209Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T16:05:36.575789839Z","close_reason":"Created new schema files (status-details.json, prune-details.json, repos-details.json, config-details.json, fallback-details.json), updated envelope.json with new commands and schema_version field, created test fixtures, and updated CLI_CONTRACT.md with documentation for status, prune, repos validate/discover, and config migrate subcommands.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8cg","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:50:37.780347948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -35,12 +35,12 @@ hosts:
   alpha:
     platform: linux/amd64
     connection: local
-    max_parallel: 3
+    concurrency: 3
     description: "Local host"
   beta:
     platform: darwin/arm64
     connection: ssh
-    max_parallel: 1
+    concurrency: 1
     description: "Remote host"
 YAML
 
@@ -60,8 +60,8 @@ else
 fi
 
 case "$query" in
-  ".hosts.alpha.max_parallel // 2") echo "3" ;;
-  ".hosts.beta.max_parallel // 2") echo "1" ;;
+  ".hosts.alpha.concurrency // 2") echo "3" ;;
+  ".hosts.beta.concurrency // 2") echo "1" ;;
   ".hosts.alpha.platform // \"\"") echo "linux/amd64" ;;
   ".hosts.beta.platform // \"\"") echo "darwin/arm64" ;;
   ".hosts.alpha.connection // \"ssh\"") echo "local" ;;
@@ -174,6 +174,8 @@ test_acquire_and_release_slot() {
   ((TESTS_RUN++))
 
   selector_init
+  # Clean up any locks from previous tests
+  rm -rf "$DSR_STATE_DIR/selector/locks"/* 2>/dev/null || true
   local run_id="test-run-1"
 
   if selector_acquire_slot "alpha" "$run_id"; then
@@ -236,10 +238,14 @@ test_choose_host_respects_prefer() {
 test_queue_status_returns_hosts() {
   ((TESTS_RUN++))
 
+  # Clean up locks for clean test state
+  rm -rf "$DSR_STATE_DIR/selector/locks"/* 2>/dev/null || true
+
   local status
   status=$(selector_queue_status --json)
 
-  if echo "$status" | jq -e 'length == 2 and map(.hostname) | sort == ["alpha","beta"]' >/dev/null; then
+  # Fixed jq precedence: wrap comparisons in parentheses
+  if echo "$status" | jq -e '(length == 2) and (map(.hostname) | sort == ["alpha","beta"])' >/dev/null; then
     pass "selector_queue_status returns all configured hosts"
   else
     fail "selector_queue_status missing hosts: $status"
